title: Weekend of upsets and high scores in SWD Premier League
description: CRICKET NEWS - The main features of the past...
bodyText: [{"type":"intro_bold","text":"The bottom-of-the-log New Dawn Park recorded their first victory of the season when they surprised the defending champions, Union Stars I, with a 15-run victory."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"CRICKET NEWS - The main features of the past weekend’s SWD Premier League action were two major upsets and three centuries.","html":"CRICKET NEWS - The main features of the past weekend’s SWD Premier League action were two major upsets and three centuries."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The bottom-of-the-log New Dawn Park (301/8) recorded their first victory of the season when they surprised the defending champions, Union Stars I (286 all out), with a 15-run victory in a high-scoring contest.","html":"The bottom-of-the-log New Dawn Park (301/8) recorded their first victory of the season when they surprised the defending champions, Union Stars I (286 all out), with a 15-run victory in a high-scoring contest."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"For New Dawn Park, Leegan Moos (121; 92 balls, 5x6, 15x4) excelled with a century."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Garden Route Badgers’ batting hero, George van Heerden, scored a double-century 232 (114 balls, 23x6, 12x4) as Pacaltsdorp posted a record 420/9 against Union Stars II at the Bridgton Sportsgrounds.","html":"The Garden Route Badgers’ batting hero, George van Heerden, scored a double-century 232 (114 balls, 23x6, 12x4) as Pacaltsdorp posted a record 420/9 against Union Stars II at the Bridgton Sportsgrounds."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Union Stars II were dismissed for 180 as Pacaltsdorp strengthened its grip on the competition with a 240-run victory.","html":"Union Stars II were dismissed for 180 as Pacaltsdorp strengthened its grip on the competition with a 240-run victory."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Harlequins Forces (110/7) spoiled the weekend for the Knysna Cavaliers (106 all out) with a three-wicket victory in a low-scoring match at the Military Sports Club in Oudtshoorn. For Harlequins, Clayton Manewille excelled with figures of 6/22.","html":"The Harlequins Forces (110/7) spoiled the weekend for the Knysna Cavaliers (106 all out) with a three-wicket victory in a low-scoring match at the Military Sports Club in Oudtshoorn. For Harlequins, Clayton Manewille excelled with figures of 6/22."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"At D’Almeida, thanks to an unbeaten century by the Garden Route Badgers’ wicketkeeper, Nathan Engelbrecht (100*; 121 balls, 4x6, 3x4), Sussex (249/9) remains at the top of the log with a 183-run victory against George (66 all out). Spin bowler Jesse Lewis (5/37) was the bowling hero for Sussex.","html":"At D’Almeida, thanks to an unbeaten century by the Garden Route Badgers’ wicketkeeper, Nathan Engelbrecht (100*; 121 balls, 4x6, 3x4), Sussex (249/9) remains at the top of the log with a 183-run victory against George (66 all out). Spin bowler Jesse Lewis (5/37) was the bowling hero for Sussex."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Summarised score cards:","html":"Summarised score cards:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"New Dawn Park beat Union Stars I by 15 runs:","html":"New Dawn Park beat Union Stars I by 15 runs:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"New Dawn Park 301/8 (Leegan Moos 121, Tyrece Karelse 43, Danvor Muller 31, Grant Esau 2/61).","html":"New Dawn Park 301/8 (Leegan Moos 121, Tyrece Karelse 43, Danvor Muller 31, Grant Esau 2/61)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Union Stars I 286 (Keano Coerecius 55, Jean Heunis 54, Reagon Rhode 44, Grant Esau 34, Corné Kennedy 30, Dimitri Carelse 4/19, Tyrece Karelse 2/78, Brandon Scheepers 2/55).","html":"Union Stars I 286 (Keano Coerecius 55, Jean Heunis 54, Reagon Rhode 44, Grant Esau 34, Corné Kennedy 30, Dimitri Carelse 4/19, Tyrece Karelse 2/78, Brandon Scheepers 2/55)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Harlequins Forces beat Knysna Cavaliers with three wickets:","html":"Harlequins Forces beat Knysna Cavaliers with three wickets:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Knysna Cavaliers 106 (Mesuli Vuba 37, Clayton Manewille 6/22, Givaldhino Pietersen 2/15, Rhupino Plaatjies 3/35).","html":"Knysna Cavaliers 106 (Mesuli Vuba 37, Clayton Manewille 6/22, Givaldhino Pietersen 2/15, Rhupino Plaatjies 3/35)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Harlequins Forces 110/7 (Camron Avontuur 22, Mondli Khumalo 3/32, Stefan May 2/6).","html":"Harlequins Forces 110/7 (Camron Avontuur 22, Mondli Khumalo 3/32, Stefan May 2/6)."},{"type":"image_with_caption","text":"The Badgers’ batting hero, George van Heerden, scored a double century.","imageUrl":"https://cms.groupeditors.com/img/664796a5-0d5c-44af-aace-93bddfb33491.jpg","caption":"The Badgers’ batting hero, George van Heerden, scored a double century."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Pacaltsdorp United beat Union Stars II by 240 runs:","html":"Pacaltsdorp United beat Union Stars II by 240 runs:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Pacaltsdorp United 420/9 (George van Heerden 232, Pieter Stuurman 42, Leroi Bredenkamp 41, Ineal Arries 3/81, Jayver Philander 2/120).","html":"Pacaltsdorp United 420/9 (George van Heerden 232, Pieter Stuurman 42, Leroi Bredenkamp 41, Ineal Arries 3/81, Jayver Philander 2/120)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Union Stars II 180 (André Isaacs 40, Adriaan van der Merwe 38, Jayver Philander 26, Ricardo Majolla 4/36, Anaxolo Khitshini 3/20, Liyabona Malife 2/54).","html":"Union Stars II 180 (André Isaacs 40, Adriaan van der Merwe 38, Jayver Philander 26, Ricardo Majolla 4/36, Anaxolo Khitshini 3/20, Liyabona Malife 2/54)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Sussex beat George by 183 runs:","html":"Sussex beat George by 183 runs:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Sussex 249/9 (Nathan Engelbrecht 100*, Heath Richards 50, Envor Windwaai 2/38).","html":"Sussex 249/9 (Nathan Engelbrecht 100*, Heath Richards 50, Envor Windwaai 2/38)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"George 66 (Benni Hansen 28, Jesse Lewis 5/37, Ravin-Lee Kemmies 3/12).","html":"George 66 (Benni Hansen 28, Jesse Lewis 5/37, Ravin-Lee Kemmies 3/12)."}]

images.alt: Weekend of upsets and high scores in SWD Premier League
Weekend of upsets and high scores in SWD Premier League
images.caption: The Badgers’ batting hero, George van Heerden, scored a double century.

author: SWD Cricket
teaser: The bottom-of-the-log New Dawn Park recorded their first victory of the season when they surprised the defending champions, Union Stars I, with a 15-run victory.
featuredCaption: Garden Route Badgers wicketkeeper Nathan Engelbrecht scored an unbeaten century for Sussex against George. Photos: SWD Cricket
